purpose: Record concrete correction events where attempted builds revealed boundaries.

ledger_entries.subject: MS-012K.6 manifest post-install task bridge
ledger_entries.original_assumption: A bundle could install a post-install manifest bridge by replacing tools/bundle_ingest.py through the existing ingestion path.
observed_evidence: queue ingestion classified tools/bundle_ingest.py mutation as ingestion_engine_mutation_bundle
explicit retry of already failed hash produced stale quarantine behavior

corrected_state.canonical_reason: Current tool shell does not expose a data-driven transition-state loader or maintenance transition path.

allowed_next_actions: do not retry as ordinary bundle
forbidden_next_actions: fresh hash workaround claimed as solution

ledger_entries.subject: T13 data-defined transition authority discovery
ledger_entries.original_assumption: Once data-defined transition rules are described, they can guide ingestion behavior.
observed_evidence: current tools/bundle_ingest.py reads data/bundle-ingestion-policy-v1.json only for existing knobs

corrected_state.canonical_reason: The data shell can describe the rule, but the current tool shell cannot consume it.

forbidden_next_actions: pretend data-only update activates new ingestor behavior

ledger_entries.subject: T14 approved privileged install candidate
ledger_entries.original_assumption: Sandbox HOLD plus human approval should be enough to retry explicit ingestion.
observed_evidence: current ingestor checks seen_status before any approval-transition evaluation
workflow exposes only bundle and apply inputs, not retry_failed or maintenance transition input

corrected_state.canonical_reason: Approval transition exists conceptually but lacks executable dependency closure.

allowed_next_actions: wait for direct human tool edit or existing maintenance lane
forbidden_next_actions: fresh hash workaround claimed as transition solution
